title: `podman machine`: fail `init` / `set --disk-size` when size exceeds available host storage
body:
### Feature request description

`podman machine init` and `podman machine set --disk-size` accept any `--disk-size` value (in **GiB**, per `podman-machine-init(1)`) without checking host storage. Users can request far more disk than the machine volume can hold. For example `--disk-size 102400` when they meant ~100 GB.

In [#28675](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/28675), that misconfiguration showed `100TiB` in `podman machine list` and led to macOS kernel panics on shutdown (`vfs shutdown in progress`). The reporter fixed it by recreating the machine with `--disk-size 100`.

Podman should fail early with a clear error instead of allowing init/resize to succeed.

### Suggest potential solution

Before creating or growing the VM disk (`podman machine init` and `set --disk-size` where supported), compare the requested size (GiB) to **free space** on the filesystem that stores machine images (from `GetMachineDirs`), minus a small safety margin.

Example error:

```text
Error: machine disk size 102400 GiB exceeds available space on /Users/me/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/applehv (available: 412 GiB).
```

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
